167204.explanation: The municipality should look at price and quality. In addition, we find purchasing from local entrepreneurs important.

167206.explanation: What the municipality can do itself should, in principle, be done itself.

167207.explanation: DURF has made the referendum possible. That truly gives people a voice. A citizens' assembly does not do that and has no democratic mandate.

167208.explanation: The government must be religiously neutral. The official prayer currently is not. Therefore, we want the mayor to start from now on by emphasizing the importance of our democracy, freedom, and constitution – followed by a brief moment of silence, so everyone can begin the council meeting in their own way.

167209.explanation: Young people must be able to participate. DURF always supports initiatives that contribute to that.

167210.explanation: The rise in property tax is because national politics are cutting municipal budgets, and our residents are paying the price. For this year, an additional increase in property tax has been prevented. We want to maintain that for the coming period. Lowering it is not realistic. That is the honest story.

167211.explanation: The dog tax should be abolished in the long term. As a first step, we believe that the revenues from the dog tax should not exceed the expenses for facilities such as dog walking areas and combating (dog) nuisance.

167212.explanation: Holidaymakers already know how to find Katwijk well. Further promotion is the responsibility of entrepreneurs and landlords.

167213.explanation: There is already sufficient space for tourism. DURF wants to focus on a high-quality knowledge economy by strengthening the economic cluster in Valkenburg, as an extension of the Bio Science Park.

167214.explanation: Hospitality entrepreneurs should be given more space. DURF stands for a lively and pleasant Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg, and we will continue to work towards that.

167215.explanation: DURF believes that entrepreneurs should be allowed to open their shops on Sundays. This should take place outside church hours and entrepreneurs should make good agreements among themselves, for example about the number of shopping Sundays.

167216.explanation: DURF does not want the municipality to pursue additional and expensive climate policies.

167217.explanation: Labor migrants should not put extra pressure on our scarce housing. Moreover, companies must take good care of their employees.

167219.explanation: Learning the language is crucial to participate and find work. Everyone must make an effort to learn Dutch. If people choose to speak another language at home, that is a personal choice for which parents are responsible.

167220.explanation: DURF wants to close the asylum seekers center in the long term and until then significantly reduce the number of asylum seekers. We have done enough and have taken our responsibility.

167221.explanation: DURF supports our events and wants to provide extra space where possible. We choose more liveliness in Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg.

167222.explanation: We do no more than legally required. Affordable housing is the goal and extra measures make homes more expensive for starters.

167224.explanation: This is a responsibility of entrepreneurs to ensure.

167226.explanation: DURF is against unfeasible climate targets. No one should be forced to stop using gas. We focus on insulation.

167227.explanation: Sport is healthy and teaches children to collaborate and make friends. Every child should have the opportunity to participate.

167228.explanation: Especially for a coastal municipality, it is important that everyone can swim. Everyone should be able to obtain diploma A through school swimming.

167229.explanation: DURF believes that musical education is a responsibility of parents, who should pay for it themselves.

167230.explanation: DURF is proud of our identity. It is important that young people are introduced to our local history, culture, and democracy.

167232.explanation: It is important that young people have breakfast so they can concentrate better at school. In principle, parents are responsible for this.

167233.explanation: Police and enforcement face staffing shortages. At the moment, there is no reason to deploy extra enforcement officers in the nightlife area.

167234.explanation: A coffeeshop is not desirable in our municipality.

167235.explanation: The demonstration on Voorstraat should have been banned.

167236.explanation: We must keep our streets livable and safe. Public space is not a sleeping place; there is shelter for the homeless.

167237.explanation: If possible, we will not hesitate to use camera surveillance, for example to combat bicycle theft.

167238.explanation: DURF has always been in favor of a green and undeveloped Kwakelwei. Our villages deserve a beautiful park at the heart of the municipality.

167239.explanation: We mainly build for our own young people, families, and the elderly. Therefore, it is good to give them priority.

167240.explanation: When demolishing and building anew, at least the same number of homes must always be rebuilt. For larger developments, a more balanced distribution of owner-occupied and rental housing can be considered.

167241.explanation: Entrepreneurs should be given more space, also on the beach, to conduct business. However, there is no room for new beach houses and beach tents.

167242.explanation: DURF wants more affordable housing in Valkenhorst. Affordable owner-occupied and social rental homes are desperately needed.

167243.explanation: No obstacles to housing construction. We must build higher if we really want to build more affordably. That is the honest story. We only do this where it fits the environment.

167244.explanation: Houses are for living in, not for investors and speculators to make a profit.

167245.explanation: Many Katwijk residents stay in summer houses due to the housing shortage. This should be enabled in a safe manner.

167246.explanation: DURF wants fewer obstacles to housing construction. There are already too many rules frustrating construction.

167247.explanation: DURF has always supported an empty property fine when it can be proven that a property consciously remains vacant. This strengthens our local economy.

167248.explanation: The threshold was raised a few years ago by the council to 130 percent of the social minimum. The middle class is also financially strained and has no entitlement to benefits.

167249.explanation: DURF wants people receiving benefits to give something back to society.

167250.explanation: Learning the language is crucial to participate and find work. Newcomers who do not make an effort should have their benefits reduced.

167251.explanation: DURF initiated the oral care caravan and the increase of poverty schemes. Good oral care saves a lot of costs in the long term.

167252.explanation: There is already a scheme to financially support people in this. We want to maintain it.

167253.explanation: This is safer for cyclists and promotes the flow of (car) traffic.

167254.explanation: Traffic flow is important in a busy and built-up environment. We do not see any roads suitable to be made into bicycle streets.

167255.explanation: The current regulated parking system meets the needs in Katwijk aan Zee so residents can always find a place for their car.

167256.explanation: DURF opposes extra environmental and climate requirements from the municipality that cost money and complicate matters for entrepreneurs, residents, and visitors.

167257.explanation: It is important to preserve the current parking spaces as much as possible.

167258.explanation: DURF opposes the expansion of regulated parking unless residents indicate that they want it.

167260.explanation: Entrepreneurs can be encouraged to do this, but it is not the municipality's role beyond that.

167261.explanation: This does not necessarily make traffic safer, but it can lead to worse accessibility and longer travel times.

167263.explanation: Healthcare has become enormously complex due to the large number of providers. This must be scaled back to a few reliable partners.

167264.explanation: Because our municipality is densely built, we have no space for a separate residence without nearby residents experiencing nuisance. DURF wants people with mental health problems to be evicted more quickly. Regionally, we are looking for a suitable location outside our municipality.

167265.explanation: Once a year, the rainbow flag is flown at the town hall. For us, that is sufficient. Symbolic politics will not improve acceptance. We are not in favor of this, even regarding the installation of a rainbow crosswalk.

167266.explanation: A divorce causes personal suffering as well as many social costs, such as additional pressure on the housing market. Relationship therapy can help prevent this.

167204.explanation: Of course, the CDA supports measures to preserve the earth. But that is not the only thing that matters. We also find it important that the municipality procures from companies in our villages or from the region. That too is sustainable: it reduces car kilometers and supports local entrepreneurs.

167206.explanation: Collaboration is nice and meaningful, and necessary in some areas. At the same time, all those partnerships mean a lot of meetings, high costs, and decision-making by others than ourselves. The CDA believes in strong local governance. In Katwijk, we are perfectly capable of managing our own affairs well.

167207.explanation: If there is one issue we are critical of regarding the current council, it is the participation and involvement of our residents. Time and again, the council has seriously failed in this. So we gladly embrace a citizens' council. Good measures are made together with our residents.

167208.explanation: The official prayer is a tradition that fits our villages. You may not believe in a higher power. Even then, these words can inspire you. It contributes to the awareness that we are dependent—on God, on each other—to shape our society. That is worth preserving!

167209.explanation: We support every initiative from residents to strengthen cohesion in our villages. So also when young people want to organize an event or neighborhood project. The CDA is the party that believes in the power of society and is open to initiatives from the community.

167210.explanation: The current council has repeatedly raised taxes, significantly more than inflation. And life is already so expensive. The CDA stands for a frugal government that is cautious about levying extra taxes. Because at home you wouldn't waste money either.

167211.explanation: Proceeds from the dog tax are put to good use: waste bins are paid for, and dog walking areas are created and maintained. This keeps our living environment clean. In Katwijk, the tax is very high. It could be reduced, just like the property tax (OZB).

167212.explanation: More tourists are good for our economy. That generates revenue for entrepreneurs and jobs for our residents. Our municipality offers something unique: a family seaside resort where space and tranquility can be found. We want to preserve that, even with some extra tourists.

167213.explanation: More tourists are good for our economy. That generates revenue for entrepreneurs and jobs for our residents. Our municipality offers something unique: a family seaside resort where space and tranquility can be found. We want to preserve that, even with some extra tourists.

167214.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be free to run their businesses and decide when to open. However, we are more critical about closing times. We also consider the neighbors' interests: the later in the evening, the more nuisance often occurs. Therefore, we make good agreements about closing times.

167215.explanation: Everything is a bit different on Sundays in our villages. The CDA attaches great importance to this day for reflection, meeting, and relaxation. A day of rest is important for everyone to take a break from daily pressures. Therefore, the CDA does not support shops opening on Sundays.

167216.explanation: Whether we like it or not, our energy supply must be more sustainable. A homeowner can do this alone, but working together is smarter. By bundling ideas and purchasing jointly, you are stronger. Such a project can become more complex and costly. With a little support from the municipality, you can get further.

167217.explanation: There is a significant shortage of housing. When a home becomes available, local residents must have priority. Many home seekers have been waiting for years. If a business wants to employ migrant workers, it must arrange accommodation for them itself.

167219.explanation: Language is the key to progressing in life. We must do everything to reduce backlogs. This benefits the child but also society as a whole. Because with more tools in your backpack, you are less likely to depend on benefits or support.

167220.explanation: We accommodate asylum seekers at the reception center at 1e Mientlaan. We think this is fine. We do more than municipalities in the region. Solidarity means everyone carries their part. If extra shelter is needed, neighboring municipalities should take the lead first.

167221.explanation: Events make Katwijk lively and attractive. But neighbors sometimes experience nuisance. That should be acknowledged. At the same time, it is not a party every day. By turning the last hour into a cooling-down hour, the party can last a bit longer, and noise nuisance decreases.

167222.explanation: Due to climate change, extreme weather and heavy rain showers occur more often. This requires robust sewage systems that separate clean and dirty water. It is also much cheaper not to purify rainwater first: it can go directly into the ditch. It's fine to set rules for this!

167224.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in Katwijk, whether elderly or with a disability. With financial support, small businesses can make their premises accessible, for example with a ramp at the entrance or wider doors so that people with disabilities can enter safely and independently.

167226.explanation: Let's be realistic: being gas-free in 20 years is unachievable. It involves high costs for residents. The statement says 'the municipality must ensure this,' but ultimately it is mainly homeowners who must pay. Our municipality cannot bear this alone.

167227.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate. Also, or especially, children from families with limited means. Unfortunately, there are too many of these in our villages. If the municipality helps pay for sports clubs, it is a small investment to allow that child to play together with their friends.

167228.explanation: There is a lot of water in our villages. It is therefore important that children learn to swim. School swimming has a big impact on daily education. But with occasional school swim projects, children can discover swimming. Parents can then enroll them in swimming lessons.

167229.explanation: Our villages have a beautiful tradition with music. DVS, the Floraband, the Uni, and the Harmonie hold important places in our villages. The CDA supports music education for children offered within clubs and schools.

167230.explanation: The history of our villages goes back thousands of years. It is valuable for children to learn about this at school: the Romans in Valkenburg, the Limes and Brittenburg, Floris V in Rijnsburg, the Merovech Park. For children, it is very fun and interesting to know more about this.

167232.explanation: Fortunately, most children go to school with a full stomach. Unfortunately, that is not the case for too many children. For them, breakfast at school is a good start. Then they can keep their attention on math and reading throughout the day and are not distracted by a rumbling stomach.

167233.explanation: The current council cut the night weekend enforcement officers (BOAs) in Katwijk's center. The CDA wants them back! Their presence works preventively against disturbances and vandalism, and contributes to residents' and nightlife-goers' sense of safety.

167234.explanation: When it comes to soft drugs, the CDA is old-fashioned: we don't like it at all. There are no coffeeshops in our villages, and we want to keep it that way. Coffeeshops sell rubbish, cause nuisance and insecurity. We should not want that in our villages!

167235.explanation: Katwijk is fortunately not Amsterdam. Demonstrations are rare here. And for the few times they do occur, good agreements exist with the police. The CDA believes there is no need to increase property tax (OZB) because of this.

167236.explanation: Fortunately, there are few homeless people in our villages. Often, these are people who ended up on the streets due to circumstances. These people should not be fined but rather helped to get their lives back on track.

167237.explanation: Fortunately, crime is low in our villages. Yet residents sometimes experience insecurity. Young girls and women unfortunately always have to think about their safety. The CDA supports measures that can contribute to a higher sense of security. Extra camera surveillance can help.

167238.explanation: We have been saying this for years: no building in the Kwakelwei. The CDA wants to develop this area as a park, a green lung in our municipality. With space for nature, walking, playing, and relaxing on the grass. With a ferry or bridge, Rijnsburg residents can easily reach the area.

167239.explanation: We Katwijkers, Rijnsburgers, and Valkenburgers tend to prefer living in our own village. So we must ensure houses remain available for local residents and young people. Where regulations allow, we maximally seek the space to realize this.

167240.explanation: Not everyone can afford to buy a home or pay high rents. There must be sufficient social rental homes for residents of our villages who depend on them. Waiting times for such housing are already absurdly long. So we aim for more, not less.

167241.explanation: The municipality imposes many restrictions on entrepreneurs at the beach. But entrepreneurs want freedom to operate. Of course, there are limits. Beach activities must fit Katwijk's character. But as far as we’re concerned, it doesn't have to be locked down.

167242.explanation: Houses are extremely expensive. A sensible mix of social rent, middle rent, and middle ownership should be developed in Valkenhorst. The latter two are just above social rent but still affordable for many residents. There should be options between social rental and expensive owner-occupied homes.

167243.explanation: We want to preserve the character of our villages. You ruin that with XXL residential towers. The current council likes them, as a weapon against the housing shortage. But those towers won’t go away anymore. They are also not necessary. There is still plenty of space within the villages to build without demolishing the village appearance.

167244.explanation: Homes are meant to live in, not to cash in on. If there has to be a choice between property investors and people looking for a home, CDA always stands on the side of the latter group. Always.

167245.explanation: In Katwijk aan Zee, many houses have a vacation home in the backyard. Isn't it fine if someone uses that as a living space? Every summer house that becomes available for this is a chance for a young person to have their own home.

167246.explanation: Studies repeatedly show that people are happier when there is sufficient greenery in their living environment. On warm days, trees provide cooling, the street looks friendlier, and it gives people air and space. Let’s do that greenery!

167247.explanation: Anyone who wants to renovate their home should naturally be able to take their time. But simply leaving a house empty while countless residents are looking for housing is unacceptable. Then some pressure is justified to give homeowners a push in the right direction.

167248.explanation: You hear it more and more: people with a job can’t make ends meet. Life has become so expensive. By raising the threshold for various support schemes, more residents can be helped. This way they avoid debt problems. Because no one deserves that.

167249.explanation: Those who can, participate. For the CDA, that is an important principle. Not everyone can work due to physical or psychological disabilities. Then you can often still contribute in another way, for example as a volunteer. That also helps to learn to stand on your own feet again.

167250.explanation: Those who do not master our language find work more difficult and cannot participate well in society. According to the CDA, a social assistance benefit is not without obligations. Personal effort may be required in return, such as sufficient efforts to learn our language.

167251.explanation: There are already arrangements to help people on social assistance with unexpected expenses. The focus must always be on people standing on their own two feet again. It does not help to pay all kinds of costs for people. Support must be aimed at regaining independence.

167252.explanation: There are already arrangements to help people on social assistance with unexpected expenses. The focus must always be on people standing on their own two feet again. It does not help to pay all kinds of costs for people. Support must be aimed at regaining independence.

167253.explanation: In Katwijk, we are stubborn on this point and we intend to stay that way. Many studies show that it is safer for cyclists if cars have priority at a roundabout. Cyclists pay better attention then. It costs them hardly any time to wait briefly, but they do get home safely.

167254.explanation: Just like roundabouts, studies show that bicycle streets, where cyclists and cars ride together but cyclists have priority, are actually less safe for cyclists. Cyclists pay less attention then, causing more accidents. So, don’t do that!

167255.explanation: Sounds nice, but how are we going to organize that? Giving every resident a pass to specifically park their car along the boulevard? That sounds quite inefficient. We do support reserving these parking spaces for boulevard residents and their guests.

167256.explanation: Our villages are already designed so that polluting traffic hardly enters the neighborhoods. Moreover, many residents work for painters and maintenance companies. They must be able to come home from work with their vans.

167257.explanation: Parking pressure is already high in many areas of our villages. Then we should not sacrifice parking spaces to facilities that are hardly used in our villages.

167258.explanation: In 2024, there was a referendum about paid parking. Our residents said loudly and clearly: don’t do it! The current council wanted to push through paid parking in many places in our villages. Fortunately, our residents gave a clear message with the referendum result.

167260.explanation: In many places in our villages, the sidewalk is narrow and the front garden small. Then a van parked in front of the window ruins your view. Separate parking spaces for work vans can be a solution. But it must be safe; burglary or theft must be prevented. We oppose banning vans in the neighborhood.

167261.explanation: In many places within our villages, the speed limit is 30 kilometers per hour. Car speed does not decrease just by putting up a 30 sign by the roadside. For that, you also have to redesign the street; otherwise, the sign does nothing. If you want to take measures, better to have separate paths for cyclists.

167263.explanation: The CDA chooses local and personal. Local care providers know our residents and can respond more quickly to requests for help, tailored to the identity of our residents. Large-scale institutions make care impersonal.

167264.explanation: Anyone who needs care must receive that care. Nuisance for neighbors and residents should be prevented. Specialized living places preserve peace in the neighborhood and offer tailored guidance.

167265.explanation: With the rainbow flag, we support LGBTI people. It is sad that some residents have to fight to be themselves. As long as society does not consider it normal that people can differ from each other, we will fly that flag.

167266.explanation: Parents separating is a tragedy for children. It also increases pressure on the tight housing market because an extra house is needed. So there is a lot of social benefit to be gained if parents stay together. Because of that benefit, investing in therapy quickly pays for itself.

167204.explanation: Sustainable procurement is important and deserves attention. But as a requirement, it must not become so strict that good, affordable, or local companies are excluded or costs rise unnecessarily.

167206.explanation: Arranging things together with other municipalities can be cheaper and better. However, we must closely monitor whether the agreed objectives are achieved and the municipality must be able to adjust.

167207.explanation: The municipal council is democratically elected and must take decisions. We like to involve residents, but through participation and consultation, not by a lottery-based citizen council that lacks broad representation or mandate.

167208.explanation: Especially in the responsible task we have, it is important that we know ourselves to be dependent on our God.

167209.explanation: Young people who commit themselves to their neighborhood or a positive event deserve support. A youth fund can increase initiative and involvement, provided the money is spent carefully and well supervised.

167210.explanation: The SGP stands for low and predictable taxes, not for arbitrary reductions. The property tax (OZB) does not need to go down but should not rise faster than inflation. This way the municipality remains financially reliable and fair.

167211.explanation: The SGP wants to tackle dog fouling firmly and that also requires a fair contribution from dog owners. The revenue from the dog tax can be used for enforcement and provisions, such as clean-up campaigns and control.

167212.explanation: The SGP finds good facilities for tourists important, but any promotional campaigns must align with how recreation can be experienced here. It is mainly about responsible recreation, preservation of peace, nature, and Katwijk’s character as a family (bathing) resort.

167213.explanation: The SGP wants Katwijk to remain a family bathing resort with peace and space. New hotels may only be allowed limitedly and carefully so that nature, living environment, and the character of the village centers are preserved.

167214.explanation: The SGP wants cafés and hospitality to operate within clear frameworks. An agreed closing hour helps limit nuisance and preserve Katwijk’s character as a quiet family bathing resort, so free opening hours are not appropriate. This also limits nuisance for residents.

167215.explanation: Sunday must be preserved as a day of rest, reflection, and meeting, as the Bible teaches us. Also practically, keeping shops closed promotes relaxation, social cohesion, and a healthy work-life balance for employees and entrepreneurs.

167216.explanation: Initiatives where residents jointly devise solutions to consume less energy or generate it themselves should, if feasible, be encouraged by the municipality.

167217.explanation: Before companies hire labor migrants, appropriate housing must be available. This keeps the living environment calm, limits nuisance and housing pressure, and allows space for a careful, temporary housing solution such as flex housing.

167219.explanation: Children with a language delay deserve support, regardless of their parents’ income. Additional language programs must be available through the municipality or national funds, so no child is left behind due to financial barriers.

167220.explanation: Katwijk already meets national standards for shelter. Out of compassion, we want to help refugees well, but that is only possible if the scale fits what the municipality and volunteers can handle. First, care for good shelter for those already here.

167221.explanation: The SGP believes nuisance for neighbors must remain limited. The current closing time of 11 p.m. helps preserve peace and livability, fitting Katwijk’s character as a family bathing resort.

167222.explanation: Rainwater is much cleaner than wastewater and therefore does not need to go through the (expensive) purification process. Even during heavy rains, it is less problematic if this is discharged directly into ditches or rivers.

167224.explanation: Entrepreneurs should give people with disabilities the chance to participate. The municipality can help financially so buildings become more accessible, allowing everyone to enter safely and independently.

167226.explanation: The SGP wants sustainability, but a rapid farewell to natural gas can cause too many costs and problems for residents. The goal can be reviewed in twenty years, but step by step, with attention to affordability and reliability.

167227.explanation: The costs of swimming lessons and membership fees for sports clubs are often expensive for low-income families and large families who fall outside the participation scheme. Subsidies/support can make swimming lessons and sports memberships accessible for this group.

167228.explanation: School swimming is important for children's safety. The municipality can allocate funds to ensure that all children learn to swim and can be safe in the water.

167229.explanation: Music is important for children's development. The municipality can help ensure that all primary school children have the opportunity to take music lessons, for example through cooperation with the music school or associations.

167230.explanation: The SGP finds it important that children learn where they come from and where they live now. Attention to the local history of Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg strengthens connection, respect for traditions, and engagement with their own environment and community.

167232.explanation: The SGP values good learning and healthy eating but does not see free school breakfasts as a core task of the municipality; this responsibility primarily lies with parents. However, the SGP acknowledges that poverty can cause problems and that solutions are needed through poverty policy.

167233.explanation: Safety is one of the municipality's core tasks. Fortunately, there is currently no reason to deploy additional enforcement officers. If there is cause to effectively deploy BOAs again, this should be done.

167234.explanation: The SGP is against coffeeshops in Katwijk. The municipality must adhere to a strict drug policy. It is unacceptable that a lot of money is spent on drug prevention while the municipality permits a coffeeshop.

167235.explanation: The SGP believes everyone must be able to safely express their opinion. The municipality, together with the police, already provides all the possibilities to do this safely. Therefore, no additional funding is needed.

167236.explanation: First and foremost, good help and shelter must be provided to homeless people, so that no one has to sleep outside. However, the option to issue fines remains necessary to protect public order and ensure the living environment is safe and clean.

167237.explanation: The SGP values safety, also in the nightlife area. Camera surveillance can help prevent nuisance and crime, as long as it is only used in the right places and under clear rules. It should be scaled down as soon as possible.

167238.explanation: There is strong support for a park at Kwakelwei. The SGP believes limited, appropriate (housing) construction is acceptable to finance this, preferably in cooperation with Marente, while restoring the cultural-historical Heerenschoolbos to its former state. In this way, it costs as little tax money as possible!

167239.explanation: The SGP believes that residents of Katwijk should have first priority for social rental housing. This gives local families and vulnerable people the chance to live close to work, school, and family.

167240.explanation: The SGP believes that when tower flats are demolished, the number of social rental homes must be maintained. This ensures sufficient affordable housing for families and vulnerable residents in Hoornes.

167241.explanation: At the moment, there are enough of both. The SGP wants Katwijk to remain a quiet family seaside resort. New beach cabins and beach tents can cause (additional) nuisance and congestion.

167242.explanation: The SGP wants a healthy mix of homes so that families, the elderly, and vulnerable people have a place. At least 35% social rental housing at Valkenhorst helps reduce shortages.

167243.explanation: The SGP believes high-rise buildings should remain an exception. The village character of Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg is important, and new homes should be livable, safe, and well accessible, with room for schools, greenery, and amenities.

167244.explanation: The SGP believes buyers should live in their own home themselves. This prevents speculation, keeps homes available for real residents, and protects the village character of Katwijk.

167245.explanation: The SGP wants holiday homes to retain their recreational function.

167246.explanation: The SGP finds green and nature important in new neighborhoods for livability and identity. Reserving at least 20% provides play areas, meeting places, and preservation of the landscape, without housing coming at the expense of the environment.

167247.explanation: The SGP believes vacancy should not lead to waste of homes, shops, or business spaces. Reducing vacancy requires cooperation with owners first. Only when that fails may enforcement be used to get properties back into use.

167248.explanation: The SGP finds it important that aid for poverty remains targeted. The current limit of 130% of the social minimum is generous enough and ensures help reaches those who truly need it.

167249.explanation: The SGP believes social assistance is not just a benefit but also a chance to contribute to society. Meaningful social work helps people stay active and strengthens the community.

167250.explanation: The SGP believes it is important that people participate in society as soon as possible. Lowering social assistance can help maintain pressure to learn Dutch, with good guidance and reasonable expectations from the municipality.

167251.explanation: The SGP believes dental care should primarily be arranged via health insurance. It is not a municipal task to reimburse dental costs.

167252.explanation: The SGP believes the health insurance deductible is a national responsibility, not that of the municipality. The municipality can support low-income people in other ways, but paying the deductible is not one of them.

167253.explanation: The SGP believes cars on roundabouts must have clear priority. This ensures smooth traffic flow and prevents confusion, which ultimately is also safer for cyclists and pedestrians.

167254.explanation: The SGP believes cyclists must be able to ride safely and well through Katwijk. More bicycle streets, where they have priority, make traffic safer, encourage cycling, and improve connections between neighborhoods and key destinations.

167255.explanation: The SGP believes the boulevard is a place for both residents and visitors. Restricting access to residents only would limit tourism and recreation, both important for Katwijk aan Zee. There are plenty of parking spaces on the south side of the Boulevard.

167256.explanation: The SGP wants cleaner air, but an environmental zone in the village centers could overly restrict residents and local entrepreneurs. It is better to reduce pollution in a feasible way, for example by encouraging cleaner vehicles.

167257.explanation: The SGP believes shared cars stimulate sustainable transport. More parking spaces for these cars make their use easier, reduce pressure on regular parking, and contribute to cleaner traffic.

167258.explanation: The SGP is not fundamentally against regulated parking, but this is not a goal in itself. Each neighborhood should be assessed for needs, so that parking for residents remains well arranged without unnecessary rules.

167260.explanation: The SGP believes company vans should park safely and outside residential areas. Parking spaces at the edges of neighborhoods keep streets free for cars and increase safety and livability in the neighborhoods.

167261.explanation: The SGP finds 30 km/h important where really needed, such as residential areas and near schools. On other roads without cycle paths, 50 km/h can remain to preserve traffic flow, as long as traffic safety is well guaranteed.

167263.explanation: The SGP believes care and support must remain close to residents and suited to their identity. Cooperation with large providers is acceptable but must not come at the expense of personal care, local initiatives, and neighborhood teams.

167264.explanation: The SGP believes people with mental health issues need a safe place to live. Where possible, solutions are sought in their own living environment, for example through supported living in the neighborhood. A separate residence may be necessary to limit nuisance and provide appropriate care.

167265.explanation: The SGP believes every person is valuable regardless of background, faith, or belief. However, the municipality does not need to fly symbols such as the rainbow flag; respect for people can be shown in other ways without political or ideological statements.

167266.explanation: The SGP sees the family as the cornerstone of society. Relationship therapy for couples (with children) should be easily accessible and free to strengthen relationships and prevent divorces.

167204.explanation: Public money must be spent responsibly. By purchasing sustainably, we take care of creation seriously and invest in a healthy future for coming generations.

167206.explanation: Some tasks, such as care and safety, require regional cooperation. By collaborating, we can guarantee quality, control costs, and share knowledge, while local choices remain local.

167207.explanation: A citizens' assembly gives residents careful influence on difficult choices. By selection by lot, a good reflection of society is created, allowing space for depth and listening to each other. This fits our vision of making decisions together, as an alternative to polarizing referenda.

167208.explanation: The official prayer is more than just a prayer. It is also a symbol of reflection and responsibility. It reminds council members that politics affects people and calls for wisdom and care. Some council members appreciate involving our Creator in our decisions.

167209.explanation: Young people are the future of Katwijk. By giving them responsibility and trust, engagement and ownership grow. A youth fund provides space to implement ideas for events or neighborhood projects themselves and to help build society.

167210.explanation: As soon as this is possible, we should definitely do it! We find it crucial that this should not be at the expense of facilities that people rely on. We are clear about that.

167211.explanation: Dogs bring extra costs for public spaces, such as cleaning and maintenance. It is reasonable that these costs are borne by dog owners, so they are not shifted onto all residents.

167212.explanation: Katwijk is already a popular seaside resort. Additional promotion is not needed and could increase the crowds further. We prefer to invest in livability and balance between tourism and daily life of residents.

167213.explanation: Katwijk is a beloved place for tourists from both home and abroad. To remain hospitable, accommodations are needed. More hotels support tourism, help local entrepreneurs, and keep Katwijk attractive as a family seaside resort.

167214.explanation: Freedom is important, but it also requires limits. Municipal opening hours help limit nuisance and ensure peace, safety, and livability for local residents.

167215.explanation: Sunday is a day of rest and socializing. Closing shops protects employees and helps maintain balance between work, life, and togetherness.

167216.explanation: Local energy initiatives strengthen the community and contribute to sustainability. Municipal support helps good ideas get off the ground.

167217.explanation: Those who attract employees from abroad must also ensure decent housing and living conditions for their workers.

167219.explanation: Every child deserves equal opportunities. Language disadvantages should not depend on parents’ financial situation, as education is a shared responsibility.

167220.explanation: Katwijk, like other municipalities, has a responsibility to offer a safe place to people fleeing danger. This requires support, carefulness, and proper guidance, but exclusion does not fit with who we want to be.

167221.explanation: Events are valuable but must be balanced with peace for local residents. A fixed end time helps limit nuisance and provides clarity for everyone.

167222.explanation: By separating rainwater and wastewater, we prevent flooding and protect the environment. This is wise and future-proof policy.

167224.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate. By helping entrepreneurs with accessibility, we make society more inclusive and hospitable.

167226.explanation: The transition to sustainable energy is necessary for the climate. The municipality must guide this transition carefully and affordably.

167227.explanation: Sports are important for health and social development. No child should be excluded due to lack of money.

167228.explanation: Swimming skills are a matter of safety. School swimming ensures all children learn to swim regardless of their home situation. At the same time, we know this is very costly. Moreover, the school must also want to do this. Therefore, we are mainly in favor of continuing the Sports Fund.

167229.explanation: We are strong supporters of music and culture. This is important for children's development. Municipal funding for every child, however, goes too far. There are already arrangements for families with limited income, and music lessons at school can be encouraged.

167230.explanation: Knowledge of local history strengthens identity and connection. This way children learn where they come from and what makes our environment special.

167232.explanation: Schools should be able to decide for themselves whether to offer breakfast. This does not suit every school. There are already national arrangements, such as for school lunches. The municipality should think along and support where poverty plays a role.

167233.explanation: Safety in the nightlife area is achieved together with hospitality businesses and visitors. Additional nighttime deployment of enforcement officers may create a false sense of security. We doubt that this actually contributes to fewer incidents. We continue the dialogue with the hospitality industry about how to keep the nightlife area safe and pleasant.

167234.explanation: Drug use carries risks for health and safety. The municipality must choose prevention and protection of young people and vulnerable groups.

167235.explanation: Preventive security does not suit a demonstration. Demonstrating is a fundamental right and should be possible safely. The police facilitate this. Security suggests escalation. We prefer to focus on respectful behavior and dialogue rather than extra costs for preventive security.

167236.explanation: Homelessness is not a choice but an emergency situation. Fines solve nothing and make problems worse. The municipality must help with shelter and guidance. At the same time, the beach and dunes are not camping places; enforcement remains necessary, with attention to the person.

167237.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance can contribute to safety and investigation in the nightlife area. It supports the police, also preventively, without extra enforcement officers in nightlife. Provided it is used carefully and proportionally, so it does not offer a false sense of security.

167238.explanation: De Kwakelwei is a valuable green area. We must handle scarce open space carefully and protect it for current and future generations.

167239.explanation: By giving priority to housing applicants from the municipality itself, the municipality takes responsibility for its own residents, with attention to youth and starters. This is also applied in several other municipalities.

167240.explanation: Social rental housing is urgently needed. When demolishing, the stock must not decrease. This prevents people with lower incomes from being further squeezed on the housing market.

167241.explanation: Katwijk is one of the most beautiful coastal municipalities, with a wonderful beach for everyone. By preventing further development, we protect the open character of the coast and prevent deterioration of this unique natural area.

167242.explanation: We aim for a municipal average of 30% social rental housing. In addition, we want sufficient affordable owner-occupied and mid-rent housing. This mix keeps homes affordable, stimulates mobility, and aligns with the village sentiment and livability in Katwijk.

167243.explanation: To address the housing shortage, high-rise buildings are sometimes necessary and appropriate. The Christian Union advocates for a maximum of 32 meters, with exceptions up to 40 meters where it fits the streetscape, such as where tower blocks currently stand or in Valkenhorst. This way we create more homes while reserving space for green areas.

167244.explanation: Houses are meant to live in, not to speculate with. This measure helps starters and families and prevents investors from buying up homes.

167245.explanation: Holiday homes are not intended for permanent residence. This can lead to unsafe situations and displacement. Structural housing shortage requires real homes, not emergency solutions.

167246.explanation: Greenery is essential for livability and climate adaptation. By reserving space for nature, we ensure healthy neighborhoods and prepare for more extreme weather.

167247.explanation: Vacancy is a waste of space, especially during a housing shortage. By tackling vacancy, we encourage properties to be used again for living or working.

167248.explanation: Many people currently fall just outside the arrangements. By raising the threshold, we help more households struggling to make ends meet. We find it important that support is gradually reduced as income rises, to prevent people falling into poverty traps.

167249.explanation: Participation is important. Meaningful work can provide structure, develop talents, and contribute to society, provided there is attention to customization and personal circumstances.

167250.explanation: We find it important that people actively participate in society. Language is essential for this. The municipality can stimulate and support language offerings, but customization remains crucial; sometimes there are valid reasons why learning is more difficult. We are aware of that.

167251.explanation: Good oral care is not a luxury. Poor teeth can have serious consequences for health and self-confidence. This care must be accessible to everyone. By providing care in time, many costs can also be saved.

167252.explanation: The deductible is a barrier for many people to seek care. By reimbursing this, we ensure that people receive timely help. Providing good care on time can also save many costs in the future.

167253.explanation: Katwijk is one of the few municipalities where cyclists do not have priority. Cyclists are more vulnerable and deserve extra protection in traffic.

167254.explanation: Bicycle streets make neighborhoods safer and encourage sustainable transportation. This is good for health, livability, and the environment.

167255.explanation: The boulevard is a place for residents and visitors. Katwijk is a welcoming seaside town where tourists, entrepreneurs, and visitors are also welcome. Parking policies should ensure a good balance between accessibility and livability, without excluding people based on their place of residence.

167256.explanation: We support sustainability, but enforcing an environmental zone in small village centers is difficult. We prefer to encourage cleaner transportation and alternatives such as cycling, car-sharing, and public transport to reduce pollution.

167257.explanation: Car-sharing saves space and reduces the number of cars in the neighborhood. This contributes to sustainability and livability.

167258.explanation: Permits can help to fairly distribute parking pressure and keep neighborhoods livable. But it is an intrusive measure. Therefore, we believe this should only happen where residents themselves want it and where it is truly necessary. No top-down policies, but making choices together with residents.

167260.explanation: Not as an obligation, but as a service. This prevents parking space problems in the neighborhood and supports entrepreneurs and craftsmen.

167261.explanation: Where cars and cyclists share the road, safety is especially important. Lower speeds ensure that children can safely cycle to school and that cars pass cyclists more calmly and attentively. This increases traffic safety and the feeling of safety in the neighborhood.

167263.explanation: Freedom of choice and customization are important in healthcare. With sufficient providers, residents can receive support that fits their situation without compromising quality or accessibility.

167264.explanation: People with severe mental health issues deserve appropriate care and a safe place to live. Sometimes this is not possible in a regular neighborhood. A separate living arrangement with good guidance protects both themselves and the neighborhood and prevents nuisance and escalation.

167265.explanation: The ChristianUnion is very cautious with symbols in government. We prefer not to do this, because inclusion calls for actions, not flags. At the same time, this has been policy for years. Stopping abruptly would unnecessarily polarize.

167266.explanation: Strong and stable families are the foundation of a healthy society. Relationship problems affect children, including their well-being. By investing early in support and relationship therapy, we prevent bigger problems and offer children a safe base to grow up.

167204.explanation: The municipality sets a good example. We procure from green and local entrepreneurs who take good care of their employees.

167206.explanation: Doing more things independently is not a goal in itself. The municipality must continue to collaborate regionally and stand up for its residents. That way, the interests of Katwijkers are well represented.

167207.explanation: Residents are in charge in the municipality! A citizens' assembly is a great idea that brings politics closer to the people.

167208.explanation: If we have to choose, the answer is yes. But as the PvdA, we want to respect that people believe. The opening prayer is valuable for many people in our council and beyond. For us, this is therefore not a political issue. Live and let live is important.

167209.explanation: Young people must feel at home in Katwijk. Therefore, they should have more say. With a separate fund, good ideas can succeed, just like the skatepark at SCUM that was created by young people themselves.

167210.explanation: That sounds nice, but it means we would have to cut back on various social services and road maintenance that we simply need. The PvdA wants to keep costs as low as possible but is honest about this.

167211.explanation: Cleaning up dog waste costs the municipality money. With a dog tax, owners contribute to a green and healthy Katwijk. The PvdA Katwijk finds that fair.

167212.explanation: Katwijk is already pleasantly busy in summer! Our municipality does not need extra promotion to be known as an attractive holiday destination. The PvdA Katwijk would rather spend that money on residents themselves or lowering costs.

167213.explanation: Katwijk attracts many tourists. Entrepreneurs can decide themselves to start a hotel. The municipality should not prevent that.

167214.explanation: Katwijk could be livelier! That means closing times that fit the location, so you can stay out longer in our villages. Cafés can best decide this themselves, so it fits the environment.

167215.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs should decide for themselves what to do on Sundays. The PvdA Katwijk stands for freedom for residents and entrepreneurs. Of course, with respect for everyone. That is how we do it in Katwijk. We also understand that some people want Sunday rest; there must be room for that too.

167216.explanation: If you generate energy locally, it goes directly to your own neighborhood. The PvdA Katwijk fully supports such cooperatives and believes the municipality should provide good support.

167217.explanation: The municipality must set limits on labor migration. Workers must not be exploited or left homeless. The PvdA Katwijk holds companies responsible for good housing for labor migrants.

167219.explanation: Mastering the language ensures that you can participate in society. Every child deserves a fair chance. If parents have to pay for extra language lessons themselves, children from poor families do not get that chance. The PvdA Katwijk disagrees with that.

167220.explanation: Katwijk has been hosting asylum seekers for years in the AZC, and the PvdA supports that. We help people fleeing war and violence. Currently, however, there is no discussion about accepting more asylum seekers.

167221.explanation: There must be things to do in Katwijk! At parties, the music should be allowed to continue for an extra hour. This makes events more fun and means young people don’t have to leave our villages for a fun night out.

167222.explanation: Too much rainwater in the sewer causes problems. Therefore, we ensure good drainage in new homes to prevent flooding. You can also reuse rainwater by storing it in barrels!

167224.explanation: If it were up to the PvdA Katwijk, we would have the most accessible municipality in the Netherlands by 2028. All places must be accessible for people with disabilities. The municipality must ensure that existing buildings are adapted.

167226.explanation: The PvdA supports a green and sustainable Katwijk. Going gas-free can have many advantages. We do have clear conditions: the national government must pay for it and energy bills must decrease for residents. No one is forced to participate, and the timeline must be realistic.

167227.explanation: On the sports field, everyone is equal! The PvdA Katwijk wants every child to be able to play sports, whether wealthy or poor. We invest in equal opportunities for all children by making sports accessible to low-income families.

167228.explanation: As a coastal municipality, Katwijk must ensure that children can swim well. Then every child can enjoy the beach and safely enter the water. Therefore, funding must be provided for school swimming. Having a swimming diploma should not depend on parents' income.

167229.explanation: Music and art connect us. Children must discover their talents by trying new things. With music lessons, children can experience what it’s like to play an instrument.

167230.explanation: It is fun and educational to learn more about our own history. It also connects us with each other and with the environment around us. More lessons for children promote togetherness and a stronger village feeling.

167232.explanation: Children cannot learn on an empty stomach. With a free school breakfast, you know every child has eaten, whatever their home situation is.

167233.explanation: Everyone must be able to go out safely and also get home safely. More enforcement in the center helps with that. Other parties have cut night-time enforcement officers. The PvdA Katwijk wants to bring the officers back so our children get home safely.

167234.explanation: If an entrepreneur wants to start a Katwijk coffeeshop, that should be possible.

167235.explanation: Demonstrating is a right. Everyone must be able to make their voice heard! With better security, more demonstrations can take place peacefully. Then the mayor doesn’t have to reject demonstrations due to concerns about demonstrators’ safety.

167236.explanation: Homelessness is not a crime. Fining homeless people does not solve problems, it only makes them bigger. PvdA Katwijk wants to reverse the decision to issue fines. The municipality should offer help to get people off the streets.

167237.explanation: Camera surveillance is an illusion of safety. It does not prevent crime and limits our privacy. PvdA Katwijk chooses more enforcement officers in the entertainment area. These professionals can directly solve problems and mediate conflicts.

167238.explanation: Katwijk needs a village park. PvdA Katwijk chooses a green and free Rijnpark at Kwakelwei where residents can enjoy nature. Expensive villas do not belong there.

167239.explanation: Too many people are forced to move out of Katwijk because there are no houses. We want people to have the chance to continue living here. Katwijk is also building many homes, so there is room.

167240.explanation: De Hoornes needs renovation, but that does not mean affordable homes should disappear. PvdA Katwijk fights to preserve the 468 social housing units. People who have to move due to renovation should be given priority to get a new home here.

167241.explanation: We have designated areas on the beach for beach tents and cabins. Beach tents may stand year-round (including those that don’t currently), but there must also remain free space on the beach. Simply to lay your towel. So no extra tents need to be added.

167242.explanation: Many people are looking for a home, but an affordable home. We therefore want more affordable housing at Valkenhorst than currently planned—social rental homes and cheaper owner-occupied homes.

167243.explanation: We will build houses! But we only see opportunities to do that at Valkenhorst. Building at existing locations in Katwijk does not fit the environment and is therefore unrealistic. Also, building higher sounds good, but does not always mean cheaper homes and causes extra parking problems.

167244.explanation: In this time of housing crisis, we must do everything for our residents. If you buy a house in the municipality, you must live there. PvdA Katwijk wants to prevent investors from buying up available homes to make money. Houses are meant to live in.

167245.explanation: You never know what someone's home situation looks like. If you temporarily live in a holiday home, for example because you would otherwise be homeless, you must be able to receive mail there. PvdA Katwijk wants to allow a holiday home to be considered a permanent address.

167246.explanation: A green neighborhood is nice and keeps us healthy! Therefore, PvdA Katwijk wants more space for trees and plants in all new building projects. That is good for residents and nature.

167247.explanation: Buildings are meant to live or work in. If a building is occupied for a year, fines should be imposed. PvdA Katwijk wants to prevent vacancy at a time when space is already very scarce.

167248.explanation: Too many Katwijk residents live in poverty. Increasingly, middle-income families also face difficulties. We must fight poverty by offering timely help to families. PvdA Katwijk always stands up for people in financial difficulty.

167249.explanation: Working makes you an indispensable part of society. That is why PvdA Katwijk encourages people on welfare to do meaningful work. Of course, there are exceptions for those unable to work for any reason.

167250.explanation: Currently families who do not learn the language already see cuts to their benefits. PvdA Katwijk wants people to learn Dutch as quickly as possible so they can participate in society. This can be done without grumbling about the benefits on which people depend.

167251.explanation: Everyone should be able to live healthily. Healthy teeth prevent many problems, but dental care is expensive. PvdA Katwijk wants dental care to be reimbursed by the municipality.

167252.explanation: The personal healthcare deductible is a penalty for being ill that is unaffordable for many families. PvdA Katwijk does not want people to avoid care because they lack money. Therefore, the municipality must cover the deductible for Katwijk residents with financial problems.

167253.explanation: PvdA Katwijk puts cyclists first in traffic. Like almost all other municipalities, cyclists must have priority at roundabouts. It is dangerous that roundabouts in Katwijk differ from the surrounding municipalities!

167254.explanation: Cycling is healthy. To keep cyclists safe, PvdA Katwijk wants more streets where cars must give way to cyclists.

167255.explanation: We will encourage as many people as possible to park in our beautiful underground parking garage. But banning parking on the boulevard scares tourists away and is unenforceable.

167256.explanation: Sustainable transport must be made attractive. That is mainly a task for the national government. In Katwijk many local skilled businesses would struggle due to an environmental zone. PvdA Katwijk prefers to help them become more sustainable than to ban their delivery vans.

167257.explanation: Shared cars mean fewer parking spots are ultimately needed. That means more space for housing and nature. Making more existing spots available for shared cars makes this form of transport more attractive.

167258.explanation: This can give residents more opportunity to park in their neighborhood when it is very busy. But it is not an end in itself. If residents want it, it can be done.

167260.explanation: There are many skilled businesses in Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg. Together with entrepreneurs, PvdA Katwijk wants to look for places where company vans can be parked. That can be outside residential areas or for example on parking lots of businesses in our municipality.

167261.explanation: Cars and cyclists sharing the same road is dangerous if cars go too fast. Therefore, streets without bike paths become 30 km/h zones, so it is always clear that cars must drive slowly.

167263.explanation: People must be able to get the care they need. We do not necessarily want large providers; PvdA wants better agreements with fewer providers. These providers are preferably local organizations that know our municipality and make people feel safe with their caregivers.

167264.explanation: Confused individuals deserve good care. By giving them a separate living place, the neighborhoods become calmer and people with psychological problems can be guided. PvdA Katwijk believes in customized solutions.

167265.explanation: Too many LGBTI people do not feel at home in Katwijk. Raising a flag alone is not enough. PvdA Katwijk always stands beside the LGBTI community for an inclusive municipality where everyone can participate.

167266.explanation: Children have the right to a nice and stable home situation. PvdA Katwijk wants to prevent problems at home from getting out of hand by offering parents relationship therapy on time.

167204.explanation: Extra rules and requirements lead to extra costs for the municipality, and thus the resident. Katwijk's VVD therefore considers an obligation in this area unwise. However, tenders can consider sustainability of materials and methods.

167206.explanation: When it lowers costs and increases efficiency, Katwijk's VVD finds it wise to cooperate regionally in areas such as accessibility, social domain, taxes, and housing.

167207.explanation: At municipal elections, you choose your preferred representative. Katwijk’s VVD assumes that he or she represents you well and makes wise decisions. An extra body, besides the existing neighborhood councils, does not add value for us.

167208.explanation: Katwijk’s VVD does not think that the abolition of the official prayer 'must' happen. However, we can imagine the prayer taking place outside the official meeting.

167209.explanation: Katwijk’s VVD gladly encourages initiatives from society. A small amount can help young people to carry out an initiative. This must be supported with good conditions.

167210.explanation: In recent years, local taxes in Katwijk have risen sharply. Whereas we used to be among the cheapest municipalities in the country, we have quickly dropped to position 286 out of 342 municipalities. Katwijk’s VVD found and finds these tax increases unacceptable.

167211.explanation: In Katwijk, dog owners pay the highest rates nationally among all municipalities. We find it indefensible that dog owners contribute disproportionately much to the municipality’s general resources. In recent years, we have already submitted (unsuccessful) proposals for abolition.

167212.explanation: Holidaymakers and day-trippers do not end up in our municipality by themselves. It is important for our local economy to bring our villages to attention. Katwijk’s VVD is eager to focus on this.

167213.explanation: There are relatively few hotel beds in our municipality. If there is interest, we offer space for more hotels.

167214.explanation: In principle, Katwijk’s VVD believes entrepreneurs should decide when to open or close their doors. We do take the environment into account.

167215.explanation: Katwijk is the largest municipality in the Netherlands (in terms of inhabitants) where mandatory Sunday shop closures apply. This is unfair to entrepreneurs. We also highly value the freedom of residents to decide how they spend their day, including on Sundays.

167216.explanation: The Katwijk VVD believes that the initiative in this should remain with the residents themselves. We do not consider an (additional) local government contribution a necessary municipal task.

167217.explanation: The Katwijk VVD considers extra local regulations regarding this unwise. They hinder entrepreneurs and are practically difficult to enforce.

167219.explanation: A good understanding of the Dutch language is essential to participate in society. The local government will continue the already existing support in this area for cases where this is necessary.

167220.explanation: Our municipality has long provided its fair share in asylum seeker reception. If demand increases, it is up to other municipalities to respond accordingly.

167221.explanation: Our municipality could use a bit more liveliness and buzz, also at outdoor events. The Katwijk VVD offers space for this, while taking the surroundings into account.

167222.explanation: Separating rainwater and sewage water is wise and already happens. But the Katwijk VVD opposes increasing obligations for developers, who already have to comply with many rules when building new homes. Additional local regulations will further delay housing construction.

167224.explanation: The Katwijk VVD places great value on the accessibility of buildings. At least public buildings, but also for example bus stops, must be accessible to travelers. We do not see providing extra financial help to entrepreneurs in this regard as a municipal task.

167226.explanation: The Katwijk VVD does not see this as a municipal responsibility and follows the policy set by the national government.

167227.explanation: No child should be left out. We therefore provide financial support for children's sports if their parents cannot afford it.

167228.explanation: The mandatory introduction of school swimming comes at the expense of basic skills like math, language, and citizenship. These have been under pressure in primary schools for years. Furthermore, it leads to a significant increase in local taxes.

167229.explanation: The Katwijk VVD does not see this as a necessary municipal task.

167230.explanation: There has already been great pressure on primary schools for years to allocate sufficient time to basic skills like math, language, and citizenship. Therefore, we do not add extra tasks here.

167232.explanation: The Katwijk VVD does not see providing a free breakfast for everyone as a necessary municipal task. In the vast majority of cases, it is also not needed. In exceptional cases, the municipality can temporarily intervene.

167233.explanation: The Katwijk VVD places great value on the safety of our villages. Municipal enforcement officers have demonstrated in recent years that they contribute to this. Within the existing staffing, we will make room for the (re)introduction of this deployment.

167234.explanation: The Katwijk VVD does not wish to normalize or lower the threshold for drug use more than it sometimes already appears. Therefore, we do not support the establishment of a coffeeshop in any of our villages.

167235.explanation: For demonstrations, we expect the necessary police deployment based on assessments by the municipality, police, and judiciary. It is not the municipality's role to provide (extra) financial resources for this.

167236.explanation: For the Katwijk VVD, issuing fines to people who often have to sleep on the street for sad reasons adds no value.

167237.explanation: Safety in our villages is very important, especially in the entertainment district. We therefore support active camera surveillance where the police consider it appropriate.

167238.explanation: The municipality has repeatedly failed to purchase the land of Kwakelwei. Expropriation of the land is very costly. Therefore, the Katwijk VVD prefers to cooperate well with the current landowner and offers, besides space for greenery, the possibility for (limited) housing development.

167239.explanation: The Katwijk VVD finds it important that residents of our municipality, such as young people still living at home, can quickly find a home nearby. Also, Katwijk should not be a 'reception' area for residents from neighboring municipalities where the share of social housing is sometimes lower.

167240.explanation: It is important that sufficient social housing remains spread throughout the municipality, even when the tower blocks disappear. Not all social rental homes need to be rebuilt at this location.

167241.explanation: The Katwijk VVD opposes such bans. We leave room for good initiatives that also strengthen our local economy.

167242.explanation: New requirements at Valkenhorst could further delay housing construction. The Katwijk VVD supports affordable housing but also keeps the cost aspect in mind. A too large share of, for example, social rental can make other homes more expensive.

167243.explanation: We have always been and remain proponents of high-rise buildings where they fit well in the environment, with sufficient parking and public transport.

167244.explanation: Too many housing regulations lock up the local market and actually result in fewer building or rental opportunities. Moreover, a second home can be part of the pension for residents or small entrepreneurs. We only accept limited regulation to prevent excesses.

167245.explanation: The housing scarcity is still substantial. For example, using holiday homes as (temporary) residential addresses can partly help. However, we take existing parking standards into account.

167246.explanation: The Katwijk VVD opposes extra rules that delay the building of homes. It is wrong to further burden developers with this.

167247.explanation: When it is not possible to rent out a property, it is especially unpleasant to also be fined. Moreover, additional staff would be needed for enforcement. We oppose this. We prefer to think in a positive and proactive way about how we can help landlords.

167248.explanation: The Katwijk VVD believes that municipal assistance should be limited to residents who really need it. This keeps expenditures under control and ensures that working remains worthwhile.

167249.explanation: The Katwijk VVD sees social assistance as a springboard. We encourage unemployed people to work. For those who cannot (any longer) work, possibilities like tailored workplaces for people with disabilities or meaningful daytime activities are considered.

167250.explanation: The Katwijk VVD finds it important that everyone masters the Dutch language well. If this is not the case and people do not (want to) learn the language, it has consequences for, for example, social assistance benefits.

167251.explanation: This is not a municipal task. Therefore, the Katwijk VVD cannot support this.

167252.explanation: The Katwijk VVD does not see this as a municipal responsibility.

167253.explanation: We maintain the priority for motorists at roundabouts. This is safer for cyclists and improves traffic flow.

167254.explanation: Space for cyclists is important and already exists to a great extent. The Katwijk VVD is not in favor of additional bicycle streets where cyclists have priority over cars.

167255.explanation: This restriction would be very detrimental to our local economy and requires additional administrative actions and thus costs for the municipality. With the current permit system in areas where paid parking applies, we already provide residents the opportunity to park nearby.

167256.explanation: The Katwijk VVD has had enough of the anti-car policy we have seen in recent years. Rules like these only make things harder for motorists from our municipality, but also for visitors and entrepreneurs.

167257.explanation: In our municipality, there is almost no demand for or supply of shared cars. Designating more parking spaces for them is seen as anti-car policy under current conditions because one parking space for shared cars comes at the expense of multiple regular parking spots.

167258.explanation: In recent years, the Katwijk VVD has actively opposed the expansion of paid and/or permit parking. With success. We will continue this approach.

167260.explanation: We will engage in dialogue with entrepreneurs in the municipality to motivate and encourage them to park company vans as much as possible on their own premises. This relieves parking pressure in neighborhoods. We will also explore options for creating central parking facilities to park safely.

167261.explanation: The Katwijk VVD does not want our villages to turn into slow towns where you can only drive in and out at a crawl. Restrictions like these will contribute to that and are therefore not supported.

167263.explanation: The Katwijk VVD believes that in this area we must always weigh what is most appropriate. Choosing large providers just because they are large providers is unwise.

167264.explanation: For issues like these, appropriate help is essential. Care can be better provided when people live together. The Katwijk VVD can therefore support an initiative like this.

167265.explanation: It should be normal to be able to be yourself, even in our municipality. Unfortunately, this is still not always the case. As long as it is needed, we will continue to provide space for an initiative like the rainbow flag for residents who draw support from it.

167266.explanation: The Katwijk VVD does not see it as the municipality's task to intervene in this.

167204.explanation: GemeenteBelangen believes the municipality should purchase sustainably and thereby encourage companies that contribute to the circular economy. We provide space for small-scale, innovative entrepreneurs and preferably choose local businesses to invest in our own community.

167206.explanation: GemeenteBelangen sees advantages in collaboration with other municipalities. If this can also be done efficiently and effectively in-house, but not at a higher cost, that is our preference.

167207.explanation: GemeenteBelangen believes citizen participation really needs to improve. We therefore want to actively involve residents in major issues. We do this through citizens' assemblies: a group of residents selected by lottery. The municipality takes that advice seriously and uses it when making decisions.

167208.explanation: GemeenteBelangen applies the principle that church and state are separate. We do not mind if the tradition of the official prayer remains in the council if the majority of the council considers this important.

167209.explanation: GemeenteBelangen wants to establish a youth fund. We are open to ideas from young people and want to provide financial space for them.

167210.explanation: GemeenteBelangen is certainly in favor of lowering the property tax (OZB), but this is one of the few levers a municipality can use. If we seriously want to try not to raise the OZB, we must choose how we spend our community money.

167211.explanation: GemeenteBelangen considers the dog tax unfair, outdated, and poorly justified. The money does not go to dog facilities but to the general fund. Moreover, municipalities have to check whether people have registered their dogs, which leads to bureaucracy and extra costs.

167212.explanation: GemeenteBelangen opts for a lean, agile government with clear priorities. If we seriously want to try not to raise the OZB, we must choose how we spend our community money.

167213.explanation: For GemeenteBelangen, more beds in our municipality are not a problem, but they must be spread across the municipality. We should not further build up the center/boulevard. Livability must be taken into account.

167214.explanation: The extension of the flexible closing time from 1 to 2 a.m. has caused more nuisance in the area. GemeenteBelangen therefore wants to return to the original 1 a.m. flexible closing time.

167215.explanation: Sunday is a day of rest for some and a moment to go out for others. GemeenteBelangen respects both. Therefore, we believe shopkeepers should be free to decide whether to open on Sundays. This way, we provide space for entrepreneurs and for residents who want to do their shopping on Sunday.

167216.explanation: There are already various subsidies for sustainable initiatives. The municipality should support and encourage residents in this. GemeenteBelangen considers it important that these are initiatives of sufficient scale, because otherwise municipal costs often do not justify the effect.

167217.explanation: GemeenteBelangen does not want to expand businesses that require many labor migrants. They put extra pressure on the housing market, infrastructure, and facilities. We want to prevent Katwijk residents from no longer being able to get a home and want to build a sustainable and stable community.

167219.explanation: Speaking Dutch well is very important to participate in society. GemeenteBelangen ensures everyone has access to language classes and (volunteer) work, so they can also actively participate in Katwijk.

167220.explanation: Katwijk has had a permanent asylum seekers center (AZC) for years where asylum seekers are accommodated according to national standards, but for GemeenteBelangen, we have fulfilled our task and other municipalities should also now contribute.

167221.explanation: GemeenteBelangen supports a generous event policy, but allowing ALL outdoor events to produce sound until midnight goes too far. This requires a permit and can be assessed case by case to minimize neighborhood nuisance.

167222.explanation: The sewer system can no longer handle the current water supply with climate change and associated extreme weather conditions. GemeenteBelangen believes a separate sewer system is better for water drainage.
