167224.explanation: GemeenteBelangen opts for a lean, agile government with clear priorities. Financially helping to make buildings accessible is not the municipality's task. If we want to seriously try not to raise the OZB, we must choose how we spend our community money.

167226.explanation: GemeenteBelangen wants Katwijk to comply with national rules to remove gas heating from homes by 2050. We consider it important that this remains affordable at all times.

167227.explanation: In Katwijk, a scheme already exists for residents with low incomes to help pay for sports club memberships. GemeenteBelangen wants to continue this scheme.

167228.explanation: GemeenteBelangen opts for a lean, agile government with clear priorities. We do not consider municipally funded school swimming a task for the government. There is a scheme to assist residents with low incomes with the costs.

167229.explanation: This statement can be read in two ways; fully paying for a music education at a music school is not considered a municipal task by GemeenteBelangen. However, when it comes to general musical education at schools, it is something the municipality can encourage and support.

167230.explanation: The lesson content at school is not something the municipality should interfere with. GemeenteBelangen does not find it necessary to teach the history of Katwijk. If we want to avoid raising the OZB, we must carefully choose what we spend money on.

167232.explanation: For GemeenteBelangen, this is the parents' own responsibility. Schools can notice if children are not having a proper breakfast and discuss this with the parents. But it is not a municipal task.

167233.explanation: Katwijk currently has five Special Enforcement Officers (BOAs). GemeenteBelangen finds this too few for a municipality with 65,000 residents, especially considering the expected growth to 80,000 residents. We therefore want more enforcement officers on the streets to ensure more supervision and a safer feeling.

167234.explanation: We see no reason to reject a coffeeshop outright. At the same time, GemeenteBelangen considers public order and livability important. A coffeeshop is negotiable for us if it does not cause nuisance in the area.

167235.explanation: GemeenteBelangen believes everyone must be free and safe to demonstrate; that is a significant right in the Netherlands. But security is the responsibility of the police; the municipality cannot spend more money to gain more attention. Of course, dialogue with police and justice remains ongoing.

167236.explanation: Homeless people often have little to no money. GemeenteBelangen finds that these people should not be punished with fines they cannot pay. Engage in conversation and help them on their way.

167237.explanation: The feeling of safety must increase. Whether more cameras contribute to this is not entirely clear. GemeenteBelangen does want more enforcement officers on the streets to ensure more supervision and a safer feeling.

167238.explanation: GemeenteBelangen sticks to its position: the Kwakelwei remains green. We do not want construction in this valuable green area but rather want to create space for a park.

167239.explanation: Home seekers from the municipality already have priority for social rental homes. For GemeenteBelangen, this remains the case.

167240.explanation: GemeenteBelangen believes that in this heated and tight housing market, the issue is not to build fewer homes but to build as many, and especially affordable homes.

167241.explanation: Current beach policy prevents additional beach houses or beach tents from being placed. For GemeenteBelangen, this remains the case.

167242.explanation: The current plans for Valkenhorst include 30% social rental homes; more is always better, of course. The entire plan must remain feasible. GemeenteBelangen will continue to oversee achieving as many affordable homes as possible in Valkenhorst.

167243.explanation: High-rise buildings can help build more homes. For GemeenteBelangen, it is possible to build higher in locations where this fits the environmental image.

167244.explanation: The owner-occupancy obligation is already applied in Katwijk. GemeenteBelangen wants to investigate how we can further expand this, just like in other municipalities. In some municipalities, for example, people from outside are only allowed to buy more expensive homes.

167245.explanation: There is high demand for housing. At the same time, new construction takes time. That is why GemeenteBelangen wants to make better use of existing buildings. We enable summer cottages to be permanently inhabited. We also make it easier to split properties or convert vacant buildings.

167246.explanation: GemeenteBelangen is the greenest party in Katwijk. This measure is a good start to making our living environment a bit greener; of course, more is always welcome.

167247.explanation: For GemeenteBelangen, the vacancy fine is a good tool to combat empty shops in the shopping street.

167248.explanation: Our community cares for each other. Naturally, this measure costs the municipality money, but GemeenteBelangen believes that we must look after the less fortunate and help where possible.

167249.explanation: Social assistance is not always a choice. Therefore, GemeenteBelangen believes that a reciprocal obligation is fine, but no penalties should follow if someone cannot fulfill it. The goal is participation, not punishment.

167250.explanation: Language is important for participation, but what is not fast enough? There must be a clear deadline. Moreover, GemeenteBelangen finds it polarizing to portray people on social assistance as those who do not yet speak good Dutch.

167251.explanation: The municipality has its own health insurance for residents with lower incomes. This may be continued according to GemeenteBelangen.

167252.explanation: The municipality has its own health insurance for residents with lower incomes. This may be continued according to GemeenteBelangen.

167253.explanation: More space for bicycles also means attention to the comfort of cyclists. Having to dismount repeatedly at roundabouts or traffic lights reduces cycling enjoyment. Uniformity across all municipalities will make it safer for all road users.

167254.explanation: Katwijk is growing, and so is the pressure on public spaces. GemeenteBelangen wants everyone to be able to move around well. Therefore, we focus on: more space for walking, cycling, shared mobility, and public transport. Cars remain welcome but will have a smaller role in the street scene.

167255.explanation: GemeenteBelangen finds it important that everyone can park on the Boulevard, not only residents of Katwijk. Moreover, enforcement is too complicated as long as paid parking does not apply throughout Katwijk.

167256.explanation: GemeenteBelangen values a healthy living environment; this means not only more greenery but also less air pollution and noise nuisance.

167257.explanation: One shared car replaces up to fifteen regular cars. This reduces parking pressure and cleans the air. Together with residents, GemeenteBelangen wants to determine where the shared cars will be located.

167258.explanation: Neighborhoods must be livable, so the role of cars in the street is less important, but of course cars are still needed. For GemeenteBelangen, more parking permit spots can only be created if the neighborhood itself supports this.

167260.explanation: The Municipal Ordinance (APV) already contains rules for large buses that are not allowed to park (everywhere) in the municipality. GemeenteBelangen sees opportunities to, with extra parking spots outside neighborhoods, also exclude smaller buses from residential areas. Parking permits are necessary for enforcement.

167261.explanation: GemeenteBelangen believes that the livability (in terms of safety and noise nuisance) of a neighborhood increases if motorized traffic is required to drive slower.

167263.explanation: For GemeenteBelangen, care must be effective and efficient; therefore, we advocate for FEWER care providers. We have no preference whether these are large or small providers. It's about the quality they deliver in relation to the costs they charge.

167264.explanation: For GemeenteBelangen, these crooked houses have a positive effect on society.

167265.explanation: For GemeenteBelangen, everyone is equal, but in society, we still see that some groups are not or hardly accepted. That is why we find it important to keep raising awareness.

167266.explanation: Divorces often increase pressure on the housing market and frequently cause sad situations. If relationship therapy can help solve problems earlier and keep families stable, GemeenteBelangen sees that as a valuable investment.

167204.explanation: KiesKatwijk believes the municipality is responsible for residents' money. The municipality should purchase based on the best offers, not based on sustainable practices.

167206.explanation: The municipality does a lot itself but also in partnerships. In some cases, joint procurement is better. Not all collaborations are good, but some certainly are!

167207.explanation: As Katwijk, we have focused on participation. There are many possibilities. Setting this up costs a lot of money, and not every resident wants this. KiesKatwijk does want better participation than what has taken place recently.

167208.explanation: KiesKatwijk supports the separation of church and state. An official prayer does not belong in a town hall.

167209.explanation: There are various ways to obtain subsidies. Everyone can apply for them. We see no need for a special youth fund.

167210.explanation: KiesKatwijk believes this is definitely possible if we cut prestige and other unnecessary projects. As a municipality, we must return to the basic (core) tasks: safety, good infrastructure, facilities, and other municipal duties.

167211.explanation: This is a cash cow for the municipality. An unfair tax on residents. Stop this immediately! The money does not benefit dog owners but goes into a general fund.

167212.explanation: In summer, the hotels, B&Bs, and apartments are already fully booked. If the campaign is about the winter months, this is a good plan; otherwise, not. Promotion for this period will benefit the entire municipality (businesses, hospitality, etc.).

167213.explanation: There is no room for it!

167214.explanation: Allowing the hospitality sector to determine closing times themselves, as other towns and villages do, reduces nuisance. People do not all suddenly end up on the street causing many disturbances. By letting visitors know when they need to leave, this is prevented.

167215.explanation: The entrepreneur knows best how to run their business. If they believe they can earn money on Sundays, they should have the choice to open. We should not limit this.

167216.explanation: Residents can all initiate projects. That does not mean the municipality must provide everything or always step in. If a group wants to do something together, this is usually possible with a balanced budget or a bank loan.

167217.explanation: Often, companies do not know in advance if they will need labor migrants. If they do, it is convenient to have housing for them.

167219.explanation: There are enough initiatives at libraries and institutions to improve language skills free of charge. If people want a language course from an institution, they must finance it themselves. There are enough free options from the municipality.

167220.explanation: We have made clear agreements about the number of asylum seekers that may be accommodated in Katwijk.

167221.explanation: Events are short in duration, and as a municipality, we have a maximum number of events. Give organizers the opportunity to get the most out of them.

167222.explanation: Separated waste collection is better for everyone and has benefits for purification, potentially lowering municipal taxes.

167224.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves are obligated to ensure their buildings are accessible. The municipality itself is also obliged to do this for all public buildings. The municipality does not have the task of arranging this for everyone.

167226.explanation: This is not achievable within 20 years. It is a goal, but fixing a specific date or year is not a good idea. It must remain affordable and feasible for residents.

167227.explanation: The municipality cannot keep paying for everything for everyone. Many types of social organizations already offer support.

167228.explanation: Since we live on the coast, it is essential that our residents can swim.

167229.explanation: The municipality cannot keep paying for everything. Music lessons are subjective regarding what the municipality should be responsible for.

167230.explanation: Schools already have enough rules and matters to pay attention to. The school year is usually already fully booked with issues the school must address. If attention is needed, children can visit the Katwijks Museum, Genootschap Oud Rijnsburg, or the tower museum in Valkenburg.

167232.explanation: The municipality should not constantly step in with money from all residents. Breakfast is something you do at home with the family for a healthy start! A good conversation with parents works wonders.

167233.explanation: If it is necessary, then it is necessary; otherwise, it is just wasted money. At the moment, the situation does not seem to require it.

167234.explanation: Everyone is allowed to do business in Katwijk, including a coffeeshop.

167235.explanation: It already costs a lot of money; the police are the first point of contact. If there is no solid basis for safety, the demonstration cannot proceed.

167236.explanation: Fines don't solve anything because those people already have no money. You need to look where to refer them so they no longer have to sleep on the street.

167237.explanation: No need for this at the moment.

167238.explanation: It is the last piece of green between our villages. Let's try to preserve it. In addition, it greatly helps with climate adaptation goals.

167239.explanation: Residents want to stay living near family and friends. We must ensure this is possible wherever we can. First, help our own residents get a house, then the rest.

167240.explanation: We have to be honest here. A project developer looks at what is possible. If a project can't continue because costs end up negative, you're stuck. Sometimes you need extra owner-occupied homes to make the project succeed.

167241.explanation: Everyone is allowed to do business. If there is an opportunity or space and it meets the Hoogheemraadschap's conditions, we shouldn't prohibit it. You can't say: You are allowed to place small houses, and the other is not allowed.

167242.explanation: We don't benefit from only villas and expensive owner-occupied homes. Katwijk needs affordable owner-occupied houses and social rental homes. Every home is one more for our residents.

167243.explanation: Only where it’s possible. In Hoornes, this could be next to the current tower flats. But you can't put a tower flat on the boulevard. It would look awful. So, where it’s aesthetically possible, it should be allowed.

167244.explanation: We have nothing from house speculation. Houses should not be switched to holiday homes. Our residents need the homes.

167245.explanation: Only owner occupation, no rental. If this can help a resident with their own living space as a step towards a regular home, we should allow it. The housing shortage is too high at the moment.

167246.explanation: It has been proven that living comfort improves in a green neighborhood. In addition, it helps the municipality with climate adaptation.

167247.explanation: An owner always wants a building to be back in use as soon as possible. Otherwise, they bear a lot of costs themselves. Fining on top of that is absurd. Engage in conversation with the owner about why this is the case.

167248.explanation: There are many national subsidies, so residents have enough to get by.

167249.explanation: If the municipality gives them benefits, they can certainly give something back! There's always something to do. Sitting still and receiving is not an option for us.

167250.explanation: If you don't want to learn Dutch, you are less likely to get a job, and that costs everyone money. An extra incentive is to shorten benefits so that people learn Dutch to participate in society.

167251.explanation: Collective insurances exist for that. The municipality should not become an insurance company.

167252.explanation: People receive allowances from the government.

167253.explanation: It is already dangerous now, but if cyclists get priority, we will see many more accidents because no cyclist will pay attention anymore.

167254.explanation: Look at the roundabouts. If you change them now, more accidents will happen. Let's adhere to national guidelines.

167255.explanation: Let's not make the rules more complicated than they already are.

167256.explanation: This seems unwise to us. Not everyone can afford to buy a ‘new’ car.

167257.explanation: We already have too little space in Katwijk. Let's not designate the places that do exist for specific vehicles.

167258.explanation: The area with permits is already too large.

167260.explanation: Company vans are sometimes so long that they occupy multiple parking spaces. Provide a separate option for that.

167261.explanation: I think we can all see and we all have a driver's license as car drivers.

167263.explanation: Large providers usually mean a decline in quality. Especially small providers are good at care and support because they have room to do so. Small providers can offer familiar faces to residents, making them feel safe.

167264.explanation: You have to keep residential neighborhoods as pleasant as possible. If one out of every 300 residents causes trouble, you should be able to relocate that person.

167265.explanation: It's not about the flag, but about the idea behind it: Everyone should be able to be themselves.

167266.explanation: It is not a task of the municipality.

167204.explanation: D66 preferably wants to purchase everything sustainably, even if it costs a bit more. But sometimes that is not yet possible.

167206.explanation: D66 explicitly chooses regional cooperation in the Leiden region for issues like housing, accessibility, and care. Cooperation in the Leiden region is crucial for building sufficient affordable homes, a future-proof economy, and good healthcare.

167207.explanation: D66 wants a municipality that listens, explains, and makes choices together. D66 focuses in 2026 on democratic renewal, such as citizen forums and trials with citizen amendments. A citizens' assembly also fits into this.

167208.explanation: From D66’s social-liberal foundation, a religious prayer does not belong in the council chamber. Everyone must feel welcome, regardless of religious beliefs. D66 stands for freedom, respect for everyone, and separation of church and state.

167209.explanation: D66 gives trust and responsibility to young people. A youth fund enables them to develop and implement ideas themselves that strengthen their neighborhood. It can also stimulate entrepreneurship and social engagement. A youth fund is an investment in talent development.

167210.explanation: The property tax (OZB) makes it possible to invest in facilities that benefit everyone, such as a pleasant living environment and good amenities. The money must be well spent for the residents. D66 wants associations and other social institutions to pay the lower OZB rate for residential properties.

167211.explanation: D66 considers this an outdated tax. D66 wants to abolish the dog tax to create a more transparent and fairer distribution of burdens for all residents.

167212.explanation: D66 supports promoting Katwijk as a holiday destination, as long as it is sustainable and livability remains the priority. Tourism must not come at the expense of nature, accessibility, or quality of living. Sustainable tourism can create jobs and income for local entrepreneurs.

167213.explanation: D66 is not necessarily against more hotels, but only wants new hotels that are sustainable and fit with the livability of the area.

167214.explanation: D66 gladly gives entrepreneurs the space to make their own choices. A “vibrant municipality” with less strict regulations fits the social-liberal vision of leisure and economy. Entrepreneurs must, however, prevent nuisance from arising.

167215.explanation: Freedom of choice is central. D66 believes shopkeepers should be able to decide for themselves whether to open, so residents can do their shopping locally and at their own preferred time.

167216.explanation: The party supports and stimulates local energy cooperatives. Residents generating energy together contribute to a fossil-free Katwijk.

167217.explanation: Companies do have the responsibility to house labor migrants in a safe and humane way, for example on their own premises. The municipality should encourage this.

167219.explanation: Children with a language delay have the right to good support, regardless of their parents’ income. Extra language assistance is not a luxury but an investment in equal opportunities and good education.

167220.explanation: D66 wants people fleeing war and violence to be accommodated throughout the country, in accordance with the Dispersion Act, including in Katwijk. Housing is a fundamental human right, and D66 works with neighboring municipalities to achieve this in a humane way.

167221.explanation: D66 wants there to be room for a vibrant municipality. Sometimes that may include music playing a bit longer.

167222.explanation: In new construction, D66 wants Katwijk to smartly capture or drain rainwater to relieve the sewer system and make the living environment greener and future-proof.

167224.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate. Too often, the accessibility of public spaces leaves much to be desired. This limits the living environment of people with disabilities. While entrepreneurs themselves must take responsibility to ensure accessibility, the municipality can encourage this.

167226.explanation: The ambition is a fossil-free Katwijk. D66 accelerates the energy transition and insulation campaign so that no one is left out in the cold.

167227.explanation: D66 invests in strong sports clubs and believes everyone should be able to participate, regardless of income.

167228.explanation: Swimming lessons are a crucial part of upbringing. D66 wants the municipality to investigate if school swimming can be reintroduced. Schools should be relieved of extra work, not burdened with it. This investment could also contribute to a healthier financial situation for swimming pools.

167229.explanation: D66 finds it important that a school day consists of more than just reading, writing, and arithmetic. Exposure to arts and culture, such as through music lessons, must be accessible to everyone. A rich school day offers a springboard for development and helps reduce inequality.

167230.explanation: Our municipality and region have a beautiful history going back thousands of years. Schools should definitely pay attention to that.

167232.explanation: Children deserve equal chances to learn and develop. A free breakfast at schools ensures all children, regardless of background or income, can start their school day equally and healthily.

167233.explanation: We demand the night back, not only in the entertainment district. D66 believes police and public order officers should be clearly present at night on main cycling routes, at places perceived as unsafe, and during events.

167234.explanation: D66 wants a realistic drug policy that combats crime and protects health. Blocking legal sales points maintains illegal street dealing. At the same time, Leiden is nearby, so a coffeeshop will not quickly open in Katwijk.

167235.explanation: D66 firmly defends the right to demonstrate and asks the mayor for police deployment to protect protesters.

167236.explanation: D66 ensures that homelessness is not punished or fined but focuses on support and prevention.

167237.explanation: D66 opposes unnecessary camera surveillance or preventive frisking. It infringes on privacy, and at this time, we do not see a need for it in Katwijk.

167238.explanation: D66 wants the Kwakelwei to remain green.

167239.explanation: Sometimes a choice can be made to give priority to local residents. However, the principle is that everyone in the region can register for social rental housing. D66 does want priority for essential professions and specific groups such as teachers and healthcare workers.

167240.explanation: D66 wants at least as many affordable and sustainable social (rental) homes in return. And we will make the surroundings greener.

167241.explanation: All beach sections are fully occupied, and all permits have been issued. Therefore, there is no space for new initiatives.

167242.explanation: D66 chooses affordable housing for young and old. We want at least two-thirds affordable housing in new projects (social rent, mid-rent, and affordable ownership). There is enough space at Valkenhorst for 35% social housing, to be realized by housing corporations.

167243.explanation: Higher residential buildings make it possible to address the housing shortage without expanding into green areas. These homes are needed for youth, starters, and seniors. This way, we can use scarce space in Katwijk more intelligently and better protect nature and the living environment.

167244.explanation: D66 does not want investors to buy up houses only to rent them out at excessively high prices. This reduces the number of affordable owner-occupied homes for young people and starters.

167245.explanation: The housing shortage is severe, and it is a shame to leave good holiday homes empty. Therefore, D66 supports, under conditions, permanently living in holiday homes where possible.

167246.explanation: D66 focuses on green neighborhoods and districts with space for social interaction and movement.

167247.explanation: In times of severe housing shortage, it is unacceptable for buildings to remain vacant. D66 wants more control by municipalities to promote turnover and availability.

167248.explanation: There must be a safety net that works for everyone. D66 wants everyone to be able to participate and wants to prevent people from falling through the cracks.

167249.explanation: D66 is against a mandatory counter-performance. It is important that people are guided to the labor market through education, volunteering, or temporary jobs. We are positive about social contributions if they match a person's abilities but do not want to make them compulsory.

167250.explanation: D66 advocates for language lessons and support, not financial penalties for people on social assistance.

167251.explanation: Accessible healthcare is extremely important for everyone. D66 fights for equal opportunities for all, regardless of income. This includes access to care.

167252.explanation: The deductible should not be a barrier to visiting a doctor. Moreover, deferred care causes even higher costs for society and the municipality.

167253.explanation: In the entire region, cyclists have priority within built-up areas, except in Katwijk. Intersections and roundabouts must be predictable, and D66 prioritizes cyclists and pedestrians over cars. Therefore, cyclists are given priority at roundabouts. This is clear and thus safer for all road users.

167254.explanation: D66 prioritizes cyclists and pedestrians over cars. We want to invest in better cycling connections and green mobility. Cyclists will have more space in traffic planning.

167255.explanation: Street parking on the boulevard and in the center remains possible only for residents of the municipality, while outsiders are expected to come by public transport or park in garages. Visitors of residents can park briefly. Additionally, D66 will continue with the plan for low-car villages.

167256.explanation: D66 wants cleaner air and sustainable mobility and is therefore positive about researching environmental zones. We also want to introduce a zero-emission zone for freight traffic in the centers of Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg, except on the main roads.

167257.explanation: We help residents organize shared car groups, shared cargo bikes, and car sharing between neighbors. This creates more space for greenery, play areas, or bicycle parking.

167258.explanation: Parking permits should be introduced to prevent parking nuisance as much as possible. In neighborhoods without parking problems, there is no need to introduce parking permits.

167260.explanation: Parking for professionals will remain. For construction workers, carpenters, plumbers, and installers we will make temporary parking permits easier and cheaper. They need their vans nearby. Using an app, they can provide license plate, parking spot, and duration information.

167261.explanation: D66 prioritizes cyclists and pedestrians over cars. Cyclists will have more space in traffic planning. D66 opts for green mobility and street safety.

167263.explanation: D66 strongly supports better collaboration in the region, but also supports small-scale and affordable healthcare initiatives. The costs and quality of care are the standards, not the size of the care enterprise.

167264.explanation: Everyone deserves an appropriate living arrangement and care; a low-stimulus environment and limiting nuisance is good. However, isolation can be stigmatizing. Allocated living places should be integrated, not separate. Nuisance must be addressed through proper treatment and guidance.

167265.explanation: Everyone must be able to visibly be themselves, also in Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg. The flag should proudly fly at the town hall annually on Coming Out Day. This shows the municipality stands for inclusivity.

167266.explanation: D66 wants all families needing support, such as relationship therapy or family assistance, to have access regardless of their composition.

167204.explanation: We want the municipality to purchase sustainably, but not exclusively from companies that operate sustainably. Too strict requirements can unnecessarily exclude local and regional entrepreneurs. Sustainability must demonstrably contribute to the environment and quality, without turning into symbolic policy or higher costs.

167206.explanation: Collaboration with other municipalities is often necessary to save costs and share expertise. At the same time, municipal councils too often lose control over decisions that directly affect residents. We want the council to set frameworks in advance, be well informed, and able to intervene.

167207.explanation: We support a citizen assembly for large and complex issues. A citizen assembly helps gain insight into ideas and concerns from society and often provides new solutions. It strengthens democracy, while the municipal council remains responsible for the decision.

167208.explanation: The official prayer does not need to be abolished. It is a voluntary tradition that obliges and excludes no one. We see no reason to change an existing and well-functioning practice.

167209.explanation: There should be a youth fund where young people can apply for money themselves for events or neighborhood projects. The application process should be simple and align with what young people need. This stimulates initiative, responsibility, and involvement.

167210.explanation: We want the property tax (ozb) to be able to decrease. Before taxes increase, the municipality must critically look at efficiency and remove outdated policies. Residents and entrepreneurs should not automatically bear the costs.

167211.explanation: We do not support the complete abolition of the dog tax. However, we believe this tax should not automatically increase. The revenue must be used purposefully, for example for animal welfare and facilities, so the tax is also explainable and fair.

167212.explanation: We believe money can be better spent on livability, facilities, and accessibility than on promotional campaigns. Katwijk becomes attractive through what happens on the streets, in neighborhoods, and village centers. That is more sustainable than city marketing.

167213.explanation: More hotels can only be allowed if they fit the scale, location, and livability of the village. We oppose large-scale hotel development that leads to mass tourism or extra pressure on neighborhoods. Small-scale and well-integrated initiatives can be permitted.

167214.explanation: We believe hospitality entrepreneurs should have the freedom to determine their opening hours themselves. Free enterprise is paramount, as long as it takes place within clear frameworks. Nuisance to residents remains a strict boundary.

167215.explanation: We believe entrepreneurs themselves should decide whether they want to open on Sundays. The municipality should not impose ideological choices here. Freedom for entrepreneurs also means freedom to remain closed.

167216.explanation: We support local initiatives by residents who want to jointly generate sustainable energy. Such projects increase support, keep control local, and help with grid congestion. The municipality should enable this with targeted support, not imposed solutions.

167217.explanation: Companies that employ labor migrants must also take responsibility for decent housing. We do not accept extra pressure on neighborhoods, facilities, and the housing market. Establishment is only allowed if exploitation and nuisance are prevented.

167219.explanation: We believe every child has the right to a good start, regardless of home situation. Addressing language deficits early prevents bigger problems later. The municipality should support this and not shirk responsibility.

167220.explanation: This is not the municipality’s responsibility.

167221.explanation: We believe that a fixed extension until midnight is too rigid. Events require customization, where livability for residents weighs heavily. The municipality must assess what is responsible per event.

167222.explanation: We believe water retention and climate resilience should be standard in new construction. Disconnecting rainwater prevents sewer overload and heat stress. This fits with future-oriented and practical building.

167224.explanation: We believe accessibility should not be a luxury. Entrepreneurs wanting to adapt their premises for people with disabilities should be supported. This fits an inclusive municipality and a government that thinks along rather than obstructs.

167226.explanation: The municipality must commit to phasing out natural gas, but cannot achieve this independently. This requires national regulations, financing, and a reliable energy grid. The municipality’s role is facilitating, not mandatory.

167227.explanation: We believe money should never be a reason why a child cannot play sports. Sports are essential for health, development, and social connection. The municipality must remove barriers for families with limited means.

167228.explanation: We consider swimming proficiency important, especially in a coastal municipality like Katwijk. At the same time, the primary responsibility for education lies with schools and the national government. The municipality can support where needed, but not take over everything structurally.

167229.explanation: We value culture and music, but not everything needs to be fully funded by the municipality. The municipality should make culture accessible and support initiatives together with schools and associations. Provide targeted support where barriers arise, but not generic financing.

167230.explanation: We find it important that children learn where they come from. The history of Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg strengthens identity and connection. The municipality can actively promote this in cooperation with schools and heritage institutions, for example by visiting museums.

167232.explanation: We believe no child should start the school day hungry. A free breakfast at school helps reduce inequality of opportunity and supports children who do not always get enough at home. This is a concrete investment in health, concentration, and equal chances.

167233.explanation: Everyone should feel safe when going out. We will reverse earlier cuts on nighttime enforcement. Visible presence prevents escalation and increases feelings of security.

167234.explanation: We believe this is not the right time for establishing a coffeeshop in Katwijk. Given earlier unrest related to undermining crime and limited enforcement capacity, we must not take extra risks now. The municipality should be cautious and responsible in this matter.

167235.explanation: Demonstrating is a fundamental right and must be safe. At the same time, this must not structurally come at the expense of capacity elsewhere in the municipality. The deployment should be proportional and situational.

167236.explanation: Fines for people sleeping on the street (also known as the 'move-on fine') solve nothing and worsen problems. We choose shelter, guidance, and perspective instead of punishment. That is more humane and effective.

167237.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to safety, but it is not a cure-all. We want to deploy it selectively in places where it demonstrably helps. Prevention and the presence of enforcers remain paramount.

167238.explanation: De Kwakelwei is an important green and recreational opportunity for residents. Construction would irreversibly harm livability and open space. We consciously choose to preserve greenery here over additional housing development.

167239.explanation: We find it logical and fair that home seekers from Katwijk, Rijnsburg, and Valkenburg get priority. The housing shortage hits our own residents hard, especially starters and young families. Local priority is part of solving this housing shortage.

167240.explanation: Social rentals must not disappear due to restructuring. If flats are demolished, at least as many social rental homes must be built in return. This prevents displacement and keeps housing affordable in our villages.

167241.explanation: We are not in favor of a general ban on new beach facilities. Seasonal and temporarily placed initiatives can contribute to vibrancy and quality. However, the municipality must set strict conditions to keep the beach public, accessible, and balanced.

167242.explanation: We want more affordable homes, but no fixed percentage without regard for feasibility. The mix must fit the neighborhood and the needs of our residents. Affordability is the goal, not the number.

167243.explanation: Unlimited high-rise buildings do not fit the village character of Katwijk. High-rise can only be limited and in logical locations. Livability and scale remain leading principles.

167244.explanation: Homes are meant to be lived in, not speculated on. A owner-occupancy requirement helps starters and families. This keeps the housing market fairer and more accessible.

167245.explanation: Holiday homes are not a structural solution to the housing crisis. Permanent residence puts recreation and livability under pressure. We keep functions clearly separated.

167246.explanation: We want more greenery with new construction, but no rigid norm that makes projects unfeasible. Greenery should functionally contribute to livability, water retention, and health. Quality and integration matter more than a fixed percentage.

167247.explanation: Long-term vacancy is unacceptable in times of housing shortage. The municipality must hold owners accountable and enforce rules if necessary. Housing space is scarce right now and must be utilized as much as possible.

167248.explanation: Many people currently fall just outside existing schemes. By raising the income threshold, we also reach working poor and families struggling. That prevents bigger problems later on.

167249.explanation: We find participation important, but not through coercion or sanctions. Work or volunteering must suit a person's abilities and offer perspective for progression.

167250.explanation: We do not believe in cutting income as a tool to enforce integration. Language is learned better with good guidance, clear expectations, and job prospects. The municipality should support and activate, not punish and push away.

167251.explanation: Good oral health care is essential for health and self-confidence. For people with low incomes, the barrier is currently too high. The municipality should support to prevent higher healthcare costs later.

167252.explanation: The deductible forms a barrier for many people with minimum income to seek care. That leads to postponement and ultimately higher costs for everyone. Removing this barrier invests in prevention and livelihood security.

167253.explanation: We choose traffic safety and clear traffic situations. In Katwijk, that means extra protection for cyclists and pedestrians, especially at busy places. The design of roundabouts should suit livability, not just car flow.

167254.explanation: We want cyclists to move safely through our villages. More bike streets mean calmer traffic and fewer conflicts between cars and bikes. We implement this selectively, only where it demonstrably improves safety and livability.

167255.explanation: The boulevard is a place for residents and visitors. Complete exclusion of visitors does not suit an open coastal community. However, parking must be arranged so residents are not displaced.

167256.explanation: An environmental zone mainly affects residents and entrepreneurs with older vehicles. We find that unfair as long as alternatives are lacking. Clean air is achieved with realistic steps, not with unfeasible bans.

167257.explanation: Shared cars can help reduce parking pressure. The municipality must provide space where this demonstrably works. This contributes to accessibility without forcing car ownership.

167258.explanation: Parking permits are essentially a form of paid parking. We oppose further taxing residents for parking near their own homes. Parking issues require tailored solutions, better design, and enforcement, not increasingly new permits and costs for residents.

167260.explanation: Together for KRV chooses livable residential streets. Large vans do not belong in narrow residential streets and cause insecurity and nuisance. By realizing safe parking spaces at neighborhood edges, we improve livability for residents and entrepreneurs.

167261.explanation: Together for KRV explicitly prioritizes cyclist safety. Where cars and cyclists share the road, 30 km/h should be the standard. This is not harassment for motorists, but a necessary choice to prevent accidents and keep streets livable.

167263.explanation: Together for KRV opts for more tailored care and support. Increasing collaboration with large providers harms small and local initiatives. Local involvement is essential for personal care and a strong community.

167264.explanation: We want separate living places for people who cause nuisance due to psychological problems. This is often cheaper than continuous enforcement and ensures calm in the neighborhood. At the same time, we must invest in sufficient guidance, because without staff it won’t work.

167265.explanation: We believe that the municipality should annually raise the rainbow flag. However, it must not stop there: Together for KRV wants the municipality to actively work on greater acceptance of LGBTIQ+ in Katwijk. That way we build a community where everyone feels welcome.

167266.explanation: We do not think this is a task for the government. Family relationships are a personal responsibility. The municipality should focus on core tasks such as safety, care, and a strong living environment.

statements.moreInfo: The rainbow flag is the symbol for people with diverse sexual orientations or gender identities, for example homosexuals, queers and trans people. The municipality of Katwijk raises that flag each year on October 11, Coming Out Day. With the rainbow flag the municipality wants to show that people with different sexual orientations and gender identities can be themselves in Katwijk.

The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
statements.moreInfo: People with a low income or a social assistance benefit can receive help from the municipality. The municipality uses an income threshold for this. In Katwijk this threshold is 130 percent of the social minimum. The social minimum is set nationally based on what someone needs for food, housing, clothing and participation in society. For people living together a lower amount per person applies.

statements.title: The municipality should fully cover relationship therapy for families with children.
At new construction projects at least 20 percent of the land should be reserved for nature.
The municipality should invest in a promotional campaign for Katwijk as an attractive holiday destination.

The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.
